feature_name: C64x+ VLIW core and instruction set
description: Fixed-point C64x+ DSP with eight-issue VLIW enabling high instruction-level parallelism. Supports saturation arithmetic, bit manipulation, and SIMD-style operations on 16-bit lanes. Harvard architecture with prefetch paths sustains throughput. L1 caches and configurable L2 reduce external memory stalls. Deterministic timing requires careful scheduling and cache planning.
dependencies: None; optional cache/L2 configuration during early init
Remarks: Follow TI calling conventions; consider pipeline and memory latency; warm hot paths before tight ISRs.

description: ~32 KB instruction cache reduces fetch latency on hot paths. Transparent to software but benefits from function layout and loop locality. Works with L2 configured as SRAM or cache to expand hot code space. Proper alignment minimizes conflict misses. Lock critical routines in L2 for determinism.
dependencies: Optional cache setup in startup code
Remarks: Keep tight loops contiguous; profile and pin critical code in L2 SRAM if needed.

description: ~32 KB data cache accelerates frequent data accesses. Data layout should favor spatial/temporal locality. DMA activity must be coordinated to avoid coherency hazards. L2 complements L1D for larger hot working sets. Cache maintenance may be required around DMA buffers.
dependencies: Cache maintenance or non-cacheable regions when using DMA
Remarks: Align/pad to cache lines; use writeback/invalidate around DMA buffers; mark MMIO non-cacheable.

description: Configurable as SRAM, cache, or mixed for determinism versus capacity. L2 SRAM can lock critical code/data against eviction. As cache, it extends the hot working set at lower latency than external memory. Partition should be decided early and remain stable. Complement with correct memory attributes.
dependencies: Early boot L2 partition and memory attribute setup
Remarks: Reserve L2 for ISRs/stacks/descriptors when deterministic latency is needed.

description: CPU-triggered on-chip transfers between L2 and L1 for low-latency staging. Simpler than EDMA and suited for short deterministic moves. Limited to internal spaces. Complements EDMA for prefetch and cache warm-up. Coordinate with cache to maintain coherency.
dependencies: None; coordinate with cache settings
Remarks: Use for small bounded moves; prefer EDMA for large or peripheral-driven transfers.

description: JEDEC-compliant DDR2 controller for external SDRAM. Requires programming timing registers and executing the JEDEC power-up and mode sequence. Refresh, drive, and calibration must match board memory. Perform pattern tests to verify margins before enabling caches and DMA.
evidence: DDR2 controller: SDCR, SDTIMx, SDRCR; Init sequence
dependencies: PSC enable; stable PLL/clock; board-specific DDR parameters
Remarks: Derive timings from memory datasheet; verify with patterns; enable high-throughput only after validation.

description: High-throughput, low-overhead data movement among memory and peripherals. PaRAM sets support link/chain scatter-gather. Channels map to queues/TCs; completion and errors via IPR/ICR and CCERR. QDMA enables CPU-triggered copies with minimal latency.
dependencies: PSC enable for EDMA CC/TC; INTC mapping; event routing
Remarks: Align descriptors; clear IPR via ICR; size bursts to cache lines to reduce thrash.

description: 10/100/1000 Ethernet MAC with DMA rings for packet I/O. ALE/VLAN filtering supports L2 switching/policy. MDIO manages PHY discovery and link configuration. Extensive statistics aid bring-up and diagnostics. Descriptor rings must be cache coherent.
dependencies: PSC enable; PINMUX; MDIO clock; external PHY; DMA rings; INTC
Remarks: Initialize MDIO and confirm link before enabling traffic; configure ALE/VLANs; ensure coherent descriptor memory.

description: Conventional PCI host/slave for connecting to a PCI host system. Configure config header, BARs, and command/status to expose DSP windows. Coordinate reset and negotiation at bring-up. Use EDMA for bulk transfers. Ensure address translation and endianness alignment.
dependencies: PSC enable; host reset; BAR/window mapping; EDMA for bulk
Remarks: Validate BAR mappings with host; confirm byte ordering; throttle DMA to avoid bus saturation.

description: Parallel host interface to access the DSP memory map directly. HPIC controls width/reset/handshake. Supports polled or interrupt-driven flow. Simplifies host-assisted boot and diagnostics. Arbitration must avoid contention with core and DMA.
dependencies: PSC enable; host timing/handshake; INTC if used
Remarks: Program width and reset before enable; validate handshakes; partition shared regions to prevent races.

description: Point-to-point serial link that maps remote resources into local address space. Configure lane rate/framing and address windows consistently on both ends. Supports interrupts and error status. Validate mappings with small transactions before DMA.
dependencies: PSC enable; PINMUX; peer configuration; address window mapping
Remarks: Bring up with polled reads/writes, then enable DMA; verify alignment of mapped windows.

description: Supports I2S/TDM/DIT with independent clocks and frame syncs. Serializers are assignable to TX/RX. Data moves through FIFOs with interrupts. GBLCTL, SRCTL, and pin control manage state and I/O. Typically serviced by EDMA for low CPU overhead.
dependencies: PSC enable; PINMUX; EDMA channels; INTC mapping
Remarks: Follow start/stop sequencing; verify clock polarity and frame timing; align EDMA transfers to slot boundaries.

description: Master/slave transfers with programmable CPOL/CPHA and word length. SPIFMT and SPIDELAY set format and timing. SPIDAT/SPIBUF provide data path with optional interrupts. EDMA can offload long bursts for throughput.
evidence: SPI: SPIGCR0/1, SPIFMTx, SPIDELAY, SPIDAT, SPIBUF
dependencies: PSC enable; PINMUX; chip-select wiring; optional EDMA/interrupts
Remarks: Reset and configure before enable; match CPOL/CPHA; align buffers for EDMA when used.

description: Standard/fast-mode I2C master/slave with prescaler and SCL low/high timing registers. ICMDR controls transfers; ICSTR reports status, NACK, arbitration lost, and bus busy. External pull-ups required for reliable signaling. Interrupts simplify multi-byte flows.
evidence: I2C: ICOAR, ICSAR, ICPSC, ICCLKx, ICCNT, ICMDR, ICSTR
dependencies: PSC enable; PINMUX; external pull-ups; INTC mapping
Remarks: Handle NACK and arbitration lost; program ICCNT correctly; assert STOP only after last ACK.

description: 16550-like UART with RX/TX FIFOs and configurable word length, parity, and stop bits. DLL/DLH divisors generate baud from input clock. PWREMU_MGMT gates clocks in low power. Can be serviced by interrupts or EDMA at high data rates.
evidence: UART: THR/RBR, LCR, FCR, DLL/DLH, IER/IIR, PWREMU_MGMT
dependencies: PSC enable; PINMUX; reference clock; INTC mapping
Remarks: Reset FIFOs; ensure baud error within tolerance; use EDMA only when sustained throughput requires it.

description: Up to 32 GPIOs with per-bit direction and data access. Write via SET_DATA/CLR_DATA and read via IN_DATA. Edge-detect supports rising/falling edges with latched status. Banked enables route interrupts to the controller.
evidence: GPIO: DIR, SET/CLR_DATA, IN_DATA, SET/CLR_RIS_TRIG, BINTEN, INTSTAT01
dependencies: PSC enable; PINMUX; INTC mapping; optional EDMA sync
Remarks: Enable via PSC before access; clear edge flags; debounce external inputs as needed.

description: Provides a 64-bit counter that can split into two 32-bit timers. Supports periodic or one-shot operation with compare/overflow interrupts. Used for ticks, capture, and PWM-like outputs via pinmux. Prescalers derive timing from the input clock.
evidence: Timer: TIM12/TIM34, PRD12/PRD34, TCR, TGCR, INTCTL
dependencies: PSC enable; INTC mapping; optional PINMUX for capture/PWM
Remarks: Choose 64-bit or dual 32-bit mode early; compute prescalers accurately; keep ISR short.

description: Main PLL multiplies the reference to CPU and system clocks. Peripheral dividers derive module clocks. Reprogramming requires ordered lock/unlock and GO sequences. Ensure stability before enabling DDR and high-speed peripherals. Timing changes impact downstream modules.
evidence: PLLC: PLLCTL, PLLM, PLLDIVx, PLLCMD, PLLSTAT
dependencies: Stable reference; correct programming order; PSC sequencing
Remarks: Follow documented sequences and poll status; update dividers coherently; validate clocks during bring-up.

description: Manages power/clock gating per domain and module. Each LPSC must reach ENABLE before register access. Use PTCMD to start transitions and observe PTSTAT/MDSTAT. Proper sequencing avoids bus stalls and saves power at idle.
dependencies: Required by all peripheral drivers; domain sequencing
Remarks: Always enable module before touching its registers; poll for completion; gate unused modules.

description: Many pins are multiplexed among peripheral functions and GPIO. KICK registers protect system configuration and must be unlocked before PINMUX writes. Correct muxing avoids signal conflicts and enables required interfaces. Preserve reserved bits on writes.
dependencies: KICK unlock sequence; ordered with PSC to avoid unsafe toggling
Remarks: Centralize pinmux setup; document conflicts; use read-modify-write and relock after configuration.

description: Maps numerous system events to a limited set of CPU interrupts via INTMUX. Event Combiner aggregates peripheral events. ISRs must clear peripheral status before acknowledging the controller to avoid retrigger. Keep ISR prolog/epilog small for latency.
evidence: Interrupts: INTMUX; Event Combiner; EDMA completion/error
dependencies: Peripheral enables; mapping of events; optional combiner
Remarks: Centralize vector table; clear source first; defer heavy work to tasks.

description: ROM selects boot source via strap pins and performs minimal setup. Loads a first-stage image into internal memory and passes control. Application reprograms PLL and enables required PSC modules. Capabilities vary by ROM revision.
dependencies: Board straps; ROM revision; external boot media
Remarks: Keep first-stage minimal; hand off to unified board init for deterministic PLL/PSC/DDR setup.

description: IDLE instruction gates the core clock until an enabled interrupt occurs. PSC can further gate modules or domains for deeper savings. Wake sources must remain active and correctly routed. Entry/exit must protect data integrity and deadlines.
dependencies: Wake interrupt routing; PSC configuration
Remarks: Use IDLE over polling; confirm wake events and masks; quiesce DMA before gating domains.

description: JTAG provides halt/trace and real-time debug. Some peripherals offer emulation-aware controls to keep clocks during halt. Debug can perturb timers, DMA, and links if misused. Boot timing and attach order can influence behavior.
dependencies: External emulator; toolchain integration
Remarks: Avoid halting during active transfers; enable emulation-aware options; script attach/reset sequences.

description: Peripherals expose status/error flags often cleared by writing one to the bit. EDMA reports completion/errors via IPR/ICR and CCERR. Robust drivers check, log, and clear promptly. Centralized handling eases diagnostics and field triage.
dependencies: Per-driver ISR/polling paths; logging infrastructure
Remarks: Unify error reporting; follow W1C semantics; include counters and timestamps.

description: No dedicated security/crypto accelerators described in standard public manuals. Secure boot or access control is not documented for this device. If present, features may be silicon- or system-specific and unavailable by default.
dependencies: UNKNOWN
Remarks: Use software crypto if required; rely on system protections; revisit if new collateral appears.

description: MAR/MPAX control cacheability and address extension of external regions. Correct attributes ensure DMA buffers and MMIO behave as intended. Works with L2 partitioning to balance performance and determinism. Misconfiguration can cause stale data or faults under load.
dependencies: Privileged configuration; coordinated with cache and EDMA usage
Remarks: Mark MMIO non-cacheable; align DMA buffers; establish and lock attribute tables early.
